commit_scope: Move executor proof JSONL into native substrate records with import, sync, and projection checks.

local_validation.notes: Focused tests report 4 passed. The migration imports all legacy proof JSONL rows into native per-run records, keeps historical pending and empty failure_reason rows lossless, syncs the compatibility cache from native records, and check-jsonl proves the JSONL cache matches the native ledger projection as a multiset. Native ledger validation reports 403 records after rebasing over upstream proof rows and adding this run's proof record. Full Form validation reports fourth arm: 204 band(s) four-way, 604 ok, 0 divergent.

ci_validation.notes: Pending push, PR, and GitHub checks.

deploy_validation.notes: No public deploy performed for this process/evidence-floor change.

phase_gate.gate: Executor proof coordination can happen through native per-run records while JSONL remains a checked compatibility cache.
phase_gate.next: Wire future proof writers to create native records first and run check-jsonl in local guards so shared append-file edits become unnecessary.

e2e_validation.expected_behavior_delta: The model executor proof ledger now has import-jsonl, sync-jsonl, and check-jsonl commands. Historical JSONL proof rows are represented as native substrate records, the compatibility cache is generated from native records, and sister north-star docs name JSONL as cache rather than coordination source.
test_flows: legacy JSONL rows import into native per-run records
sync-jsonl writes a compatibility cache from native records
check-jsonl proves native records and JSONL cache carry the same projected rows
validate proves all native proof records satisfy the ledger schema
full Form validation preserves the current 204-band four-way floor with 604 ok and 0 divergent
